content.description:
The type system in Hobson forms chains: every item has a `type` field pointing to its type item, which itself has a type, forming a chain that terminates at `atom` (self-referential) or `TYPE_DEFINITION`. The kernel validates these chains on every item save.

## The Type Chain

Starting from any item, following the `type` field produces a chain:

```
my-note → note → TYPE_DEFINITION → atom (self-referential)
my-view → view → code → TYPE_DEFINITION → atom
```

The kernel walks this chain during `typeChainIncludes()` to answer questions like "is this item a code item?" (does its chain include `code`?).

## Bounded Walking

Type chains are walked with a boundary at `TYPE_DEFINITION`. When an item's type is `TYPE_DEFINITION`, that item is itself a type (not an instance), so walking further crosses a meta-level boundary.

```javascript
async typeChainIncludes(typeId, targetId) {
  let current = typeId;
  const visited = new Set();

  while (current && !visited.has(current)) {
    if (current === targetId) return true;
    visited.add(current);

    if (current === this.kernel.IDS.ATOM) break;

    const typeItem = await this.kernel.storage.get(current);
    if (typeItem.type === this.kernel.IDS.TYPE_DEFINITION) break;

    current = typeItem.type;
  }

  return false;
}
```

This prevents meta-level confusion:
- `note_view` "is code" → YES (code is in chain before TYPE_DEFINITION boundary)
- `note_view` "is type_definition" → NO (TYPE_DEFINITION is past the boundary)

## Validation Rules

On every `storage.set()` call, the kernel validates:

1. **Type exists** — the referenced type item must exist in storage
2. **Chain terminates** — following type pointers must reach `atom` or `TYPE_DEFINITION` without infinite loops
3. **No circular types** — visited-set prevents infinite walking
4. **Code item name uniqueness** — if the item is a code item (type chain includes `code`), its `name` must be unique among all code items

## Why Bounded Walking Matters

The chain conflates two different relationships:
1. **Subtype** (same meta-level): `view` is-a-kind-of `code`
2. **Meta-level** (crosses levels): `code` is-defined-by `TYPE_DEFINITION`

Without the boundary, `typeChainIncludes(noteViewType, TYPE_DEFINITION)` would return `true`, which is meaningless — a note view is not "a type definition."

**One rule** (stop at TYPE_DEFINITION) handles all capability detection correctly, without needing per-field content inspection.

## See Also

- [Cycle-Safe Traversal](item://c0c0c0c0-0013-0000-0000-000000000000) — Cycle detection in the attachment graph
- [kernel:storage](item://33333333-2222-0000-0000-000000000000) — Where validation lives
- [Core Concepts](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000002) — Type hierarchy overview
